updates.tweet_text:
Highly precise evening technicals in #ES_F. Per 4pm newsletter, 7390 was support. Held exact 6pm. 7425 Failed Breakdown was then a long trigger. We triggered around midnight, +25.

Plan today: Ride runner. 7458, 7478, 7508, 7527 above. 7425 (weak), 7390=supports. Sell<90 https://t.co/euAlkZ71EO
updates.notes: All prices mentioned (7390, 7425, 7458, 7478, 7508, 7527) are either morning plan levels or hindsight/trade management references. No new actionable levels introduced.

updates.tweet_text: Note: As usual everyday trading should be essentially the same if you are doing it right. For us it is always: Elevator down sell, failed breakdown, manage level to level, leave runner. Now the cycle restarts and nothing to do but ride runner and wait for another elevator down
updates.notes: Pure process/philosophy commentary with no prices named. No actionable levels of any kind mentioned.

updates.tweet_text: @tamelessX The technically valid stop has to go under 7390 unfortunately which means you'd have to use a smaller size (which is what I did), or you can drag it up to an arbitrary point or under some lower TF higher low, but it will drop the win rate on the trade
updates.notes: Reply discussing stop placement under 7390 (morning plan level) — trade management commentary, no new actionable level introduced.

updates.tweet_text: @tamelessX There was a small higher low at 7415 at 10:30PM so you can put it under that
updates.notes: Reply to user about stop placement under 7415; trade management/conversational, not a named actionable level with naming language.

updates.tweet_text: @tamelessX For Failed Breakdowns though the stop always goes under the lowest low if you want the highest win rate. It will mean lower R/R. YOu can drag it up higher, but as you do, the win rate goes down and the R/R goes up
updates.notes: Reply explaining FBD stop placement mechanics — no price levels named, purely educational/trade management commentary.

updates.tweet_text: @tamelessX There is no way to get both high win rate and high R/R so need to pick one, I always pick win rate
updates.notes: Reply tweet about trading philosophy (win rate vs R/R); no price levels mentioned at all.

updates.tweet_text: @loloiuytre Just normal in ES. Always goes "elevator down" then we get the squeeze. Most boring asset class in the world and never does anything surprising (which is why I like trading it!)

updates.tweet_text: @EStrader33 @loloiuytre Has been since I started trading it in 2006! We do get black swan type events every 4-5 years though (Covid, the Tariff Crash etc). Even though though once you trade through one or two they are fairly repetitive in how they operate
